name: Effect v4 Core Concepts Overview
episode_body:
Title: Effect v4 Core Concepts Overview
Category: concept

Effect v4 (beta) is a comprehensive TypeScript library for building type-safe, composable applications.
It provides a unified package "effect" that replaces the previous multi-package structure (@effect/platform, @effect/cli, etc.).

Core Modules:
- Effect: The central type representing effectful computations with typed errors and dependencies
- Schema: Runtime validation, encoding/decoding with type inference
- Stream: Lazy, composable streaming with backpressure
- Layer: Dependency injection through composable service layers
- ServiceMap: Service definition and management (replaces v3's Context.Tag)
- Option: Type-safe optional values (replaces null/undefined)
- Result: Type-safe error handling (replaces Either from v3)
- Fiber: Lightweight concurrency primitives
- Scope: Resource lifecycle management
- Array: Immutable functional array operations

Key v4 Changes from v3:
- Context.Tag replaced by ServiceMap.Service
- Effect.catchAll replaced by Effect.catch
- Either renamed to Result
- Schema.decode replaced by Schema.decodeUnknownEffect
- FileSystem/Path moved to main "effect" package from @effect/platform
- Types that were Effect subtypes (Ref, Deferred, Fiber) now use Yieldable trait
- Layer.scoped removed, Layer.effect handles scoping automatically

name: Effect v4 Module: Array
episode_body:
Title: Array Module
Category: module
Module Path: effect/Array

Mental Model:
- **`Array<A>`** is a standard JS array. All functions in this module return
  new arrays; the input is never mutated.
- **`NonEmptyReadonlyArray<A>`** (`readonly [A, ...Array<A>]`) is a readonly
  array guaranteed to have at least one element. Many functions preserve or
  require this guarantee at the type level.
- **`NonEmptyArray<A>`** is the mutable counterpart: `[A, ...Array<A>]`.
- Most functions are **dual** — they can be called either as
  `Array.fn(array, arg)` (data-first) or piped as
  `pipe(array, Array.fn(arg))` (data-last).
- Functions that access elements by index return `Option<A>` for safety; use
  the `*NonEmpty` variants (e.g. headNonEmpty) when you already know
  the array is non-empty.
- Set-like operations (union, intersection,
  difference) use `Equal.equivalence()` by default; use the `*With`
  variants for custom equality.

Common Tasks:
- **Create** an array: make, of, empty,
  fromIterable, range, makeBy, replicate,
  unfold
- **Access** elements: head, last, get, tail,
  init
- **Transform**: map, flatMap, flatten
- **Filter**: filter, partition, dedupe
- **Combine**: append, prepend, appendAll,
  prependAll, zip, cartesian
- **Split**: splitAt, chunksOf, span, window
- **Search**: findFirst, findLast, contains
- **Sort**: sort, sortBy, sortWith
- **Fold**: reduce, scan, join
- **Group**: groupBy, group, groupWith
- **Set operations**: union, intersection,
  difference
- **Match** on empty vs non-empty: match, matchLeft,
  matchRight
- **Check** properties: isArray, isArrayNonEmpty,
  every, some

Gotchas:
- fromIterable returns the original array reference when given an
  array; if you need a copy, use copy.
- `sort`, `reverse`, etc. always allocate a new array — the input is never
  mutated.
- makeBy and replicate normalize `n` to an integer >= 1 —
  they never produce an empty array.
- range`(start, end)` is inclusive on both ends. If `start > end` it
  returns `[start]`.
- Functions returning `Option` (e.g. head, findFirst) return
  `Option.none()` for empty inputs — they never throw.

Quickstart:
**Example** (Basic array operations)

```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const numbers = Array.make(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)

const doubled = Array.map(numbers, (n) => n * 2)
console.log(doubled) // [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]

const evens = Array.filter(numbers, (n) => n % 2 === 0)
console.log(evens) // [2, 4]

const sum = Array.reduce(numbers, 0, (acc, n) => acc + n)
console.log(sum) // 15
```

See Also:
make — create a non-empty array from elements
map — transform each element
filter — keep elements matching a predicate
reduce — fold an array to a single value

Key Exports by Category (138 total):
- constructors: Array, make, allocate, makeBy, range, replicate, fromIterable, ensure, ... (11 total)
- models: NonEmptyReadonlyArray, NonEmptyArray
- conversions: fromRecord, fromOption, fromNullishOr
- pattern matching: match, matchLeft, matchRight
- concatenating: prepend, prependAll, append, appendAll
- folding: scan, scanRight, reduce, reduceRight, join, mapAccum, countBy
- guards: isArray, isArrayEmpty, isReadonlyArrayEmpty, isArrayNonEmpty, isReadonlyArrayNonEmpty
- getters: length, get, head, headNonEmpty, last, lastNonEmpty, tail, tailNonEmpty, ... (16 total)
- uncategorized: isOutOfBounds, makeReducerConcat
- unsafe: getUnsafe
- splitting: unprepend, unappend, span, splitAt, splitAtNonEmpty, split, splitWhere, chunksOf, ... (9 total)
- elements: findFirstIndex, findLastIndex, findFirst, findFirstWithIndex, findLast, insertAt, replace, modify, ... (39 total)
- sorting: sort, sortBy
- zipping: zip, zipWith, unzip
- grouping: groupWith, group, groupBy
- types: ReadonlyArray, Infer, With, OrNonEmpty, AndNonEmpty, Flatten
- mapping: map, extend
- sequencing: flatMap, flatten, flatMapNullishOr
- filtering: partitionMap, getSomes, getFailures, getSuccesses, filter, partition, separate
- lifting: liftPredicate, liftOption, liftNullishOr, liftResult
- instances: makeOrder, makeEquivalence
- do notation: Do, bind, bindTo, getReadonlyReducerConcat
Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Module: Effect
episode_body:
Title: Effect Module
Category: module
Module Path: effect/Effect

Description: The `Effect` module is the core of the Effect library, providing a powerful and expressive
way to model and compose asynchronous, concurrent, and effectful computations.

An `Effect<A, E, R>` represents a computation that:
- May succeed with a value of type `A`
- May fail with an error of type `E`
- Requires a context/environment of type `R`

Effects are lazy and immutable - they describe computations that can be executed later.
This allows for powerful composition, error handling, resource management, and concurrency
patterns.

Key Exports by Category (244 total):
- Models: Effect, Variance, Success, Error, Services, EffectIterator, All, EffectAny, ... (17 total)
- Yieldable: Yieldable, YieldableClass, Yieldable, Any, Success
- Guards: isEffect
- Collecting: all, partition, forEach, whileLoop, replicate, replicateEffect
- Creating Effects: promise, tryPromise, succeed, succeedNone, succeedSome, suspend, sync, callback, ... (18 total)
- uncategorized: gen, Return
- Conversions: fromResult, fromOption, fromNullishOr, fromYieldable
- Sequencing: flatMap, flatten, andThen, tap, tapError, tapErrorTag, tapCause, tapCauseIf, ... (9 total)
- Outcome Encapsulation: result, exit
- Output Encapsulation: option
- Mapping: map, as, asSome, asVoid, flip, mapBoth
- Zipping: zip, zipWith
- Error Handling: catchTag, catchTags, catchReason, catchReasons, TagsWithReason, unwrapReason, catchCause, catchDefect, ... (20 total)
- Converting Failures to Defects: orDie
- Repetition / Recursion: eventually, Repeat, Return, Options, forever, repeat, repeatOrElse, schedule, ... (9 total)
- Fallback: withExecutionPlan, orElseSucceed
- Delays & Timeouts: timeout, timeoutOption, timeoutOrElse, delay, sleep, timed
- Racing: raceAll, raceAllFirst, race, raceFirst
- Filtering: filter, filterOrElse, filterOrFail
- Conditional Operators: when
- Pattern Matching: match, matchEager, matchCause, matchCauseEager, matchCauseEffectEager, matchCauseEffect, matchEffect
- Condition Checking: isFailure, isSuccess
- Environment: services, servicesWith, provide, provideServices
- ServiceMap: service, serviceOption, updateServices, updateService, provideService, provideServiceEffect
- References: withConcurrency
- Resource Management & Finalization: scope, scoped, scopedWith, acquireRelease, acquireUseRelease, addFinalizer, ensuring, onError, ... (12 total)
- Caching: cached, cachedWithTTL, cachedInvalidateWithTTL
- Interruption: interrupt, interruptible, onInterrupt, uninterruptible, uninterruptibleMask, interruptibleMask
- Semaphore: Semaphore, makeSemaphoreUnsafe, makeSemaphore
- Latch: Latch, makeLatchUnsafe, makeLatch
- Tracing: tracer, withTracer, withTracerEnabled, withTracerTiming, annotateSpans, annotateCurrentSpan, currentSpan, currentParentSpan, ... (17 total)
- Requests & Batching: request, requestUnsafe
- Supervision & Fibers: forkChild, forkIn, forkScoped, forkDetach, awaitAllChildren, fiber, fiberId
- Running Effects: RunOptions, runFork, runForkWith, runCallbackWith, runCallback, runPromise, runPromiseWith, runPromiseExit, ... (13 total)
- Function: fn, fnUntraced, fn
- Clock: clockWith
- Logging: logWithLevel, log, logFatal, logWarning, logError, logInfo, logDebug, logTrace, ... (11 total)
- Tracking: track, trackSuccesses, trackErrors, trackDefects, trackDuration
- Transactions: Transaction, atomic, atomicWith, transaction, transactionWith, retryTransaction
- Effectify: Effectify, Effectify, effectify
- Util: EffectifyError
- Type Constraints: satisfiesSuccessType, satisfiesErrorType, satisfiesServicesType
- Eager: mapEager, mapErrorEager, mapBothEager, flatMapEager, catchEager, fnUntracedEager

name: Effect v4 Migration: Context.GenericTag replaced by ServiceMap.Service
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Context.GenericTag replaced by ServiceMap.Service
Category: migration
Source: services.md

Change: Context.GenericTag<T>(id) replaced by ServiceMap.Service<T>(id) for defining services with function syntax.

v3 Pattern:
import { Context } from "effect"
interface Database { readonly query: (sql: string) => string }
const Database = Context.GenericTag<Database>("Database")

v4 Pattern:
import { ServiceMap } from "effect"
interface Database { readonly query: (sql: string) => string }
const Database = ServiceMap.Service<Database>("Database")

Reason: Context module replaced by ServiceMap. Services are now defined via ServiceMap.Service.

Affected Modules: Context (removed), ServiceMap

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchAll renamed to Effect.catch
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchAll renamed to Effect.catch
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchAll renamed to Effect.catch. The general pattern is catchAll* shortened to catch*.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.fail("error").pipe(Effect.catchAll((error) => Effect.succeed(`recovered: ${error}`)))

v4 Pattern:
Effect.fail("error").pipe(Effect.catch((error) => Effect.succeed(`recovered: ${error}`)))

Reason: Shortened naming convention - catchAll* becomes catch*.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: FiberRef.get replaced by yielding Reference directly
Effect v4 Correction: Context.GenericTag does not exist
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Context.GenericTag does not exist
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: import * as Context from "effect/Context"; const FsUtils = Context.GenericTag<FsUtils>("@beep/repo-utils/FsUtils")

Correct v4 Pattern: import { ServiceMap } from "effect"; const FsUtils = ServiceMap.Service<FsUtils>("@beep/repo-utils/FsUtils"); or class-based: class FsUtils extends ServiceMap.Service<FsUtils, FsUtilsShape>()("@beep/repo-utils/FsUtils", { make: Effect.gen(function*() { ... }) }) {}

Explanation: The entire Context module is gone from Effect v4. It is not exported from the barrel index.ts. Use ServiceMap.Service instead.

Affected Modules: effect/Context, effect/ServiceMap
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: Schema.decode does not exist
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: Schema.decode does not exist
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: const result = Schema.decode(PackageJson)(jsonData)

Correct v4 Pattern: Schema.decodeUnknownEffect(PackageJson)(data) for effectful decoding (returns Effect<Type, SchemaError>), Schema.decodeUnknownSync(PackageJson)(data) for synchronous decoding (throws on error), Schema.decodeSync(PackageJson)(data) for decoding from encoded type (not unknown).

Explanation: Schema.decode does not exist in v4. Use the explicit effectful or sync variants instead.

Affected Modules: effect/Schema
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Correction: @effect/platform imports moved to effect package
episode_body:
Title: v4 Correction: @effect/platform imports moved to effect package
Category: migration
Source: repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

Incorrect Pattern: import * as FileSystem from "@effect/platform/FileSystem"; import * as Path from "@effect/platform/Path"

Correct v4 Pattern: import { FileSystem, Path, PlatformError } from "effect"; // Service access: const fs = yield* FileSystem.FileSystem; const path = yield* Path.Path; // Node layers still from: import { NodeFileSystem, NodePath } from "@effect/platform-node"

Explanation: FileSystem and Path abstractions moved to the main effect package in v4. Only the Node.js implementations remain in @effect/platform-node. You may not need @effect/platform as a dependency at all.

Affected Modules: @effect/platform, effect/FileSystem, effect/Path, @effect/platform-node
source_description: Effect v4 API correction from repo-utils-effect-v4-corrections.md

name: Effect v4 Pattern: Error Handling
episode_body:
Title: Pattern: Error Handling in Effect
Category: pattern
Source: error-handling.md

Description: Comprehensive error handling patterns for the Effect library, covering structured error types, error creation, error handling combinators, testing, and recovery strategies.

When to Use: When defining errors, handling errors in Effect pipelines, testing error conditions, or implementing retry/fallback logic.

Key Points:

Three Error Type Patterns:
1. Data.TaggedError: Core pattern with _tag for catchTag discrimination. Use for most application errors.
2. Data.Error: Simpler pattern without _tag. Use when discrimination not needed.
3. Schema.ErrorClass: For serializable, schema-validated errors in CLI, HTTP APIs, distributed systems. Pattern: `Schema.ErrorClass('TypeId/Name')({ _tag: Schema.tag('Name'), fields... })`

Error Composition:
- Use flat union types for composition, NOT abstract base classes
- Better type inference, simpler catchTag, serialization-friendly
- Pattern: `type HttpClientError = RequestError | ResponseError`

Error Creation:
- Effect.try: For operations that might throw synchronously
- Effect.tryPromise: For Promise-based operations
- Always wrap with structured error types in the catch handler

Error Handling Combinators:
- Effect.catchAll: Handle all errors uniformly
- Effect.catchTag: Handle specific tagged error types
- Effect.catchSome: Selectively handle certain errors (return Option)

Error Testing:
- Use Effect.exit to inspect success/failure without throwing
- Test with catchTag to verify error handling paths
- Use Exit.isFailure/Exit.isSuccess for assertions

Error Recovery Patterns:
- Retry with exponential backoff: Schedule.exponential with Schedule.whileInput and Schedule.recurs
- Fallback chain: acc.pipe(Effect.orElse(() => fallback))

Standardized Error Reasons:
- SystemErrorReason: AlreadyExists, BadResource, Busy, InvalidData, NotFound, PermissionDenied, TimedOut, UnexpectedEof, Unknown, WouldBlock, WriteZero
- HttpClientErrorReason: Transport, Encode, InvalidUrl, StatusCode, Decode, EmptyBody

Related Patterns: effect-library-development.md, platform-integration.md

name: Effect v4 Doc: Schema - Structs and Composite Types
episode_body:
Title: Schema Structs, Tuples, Arrays, Records, and Unions
Category: documentation
Source: SCHEMA.md (Section 2)
Module Path: effect/Schema

Description: Schema v4 provides composite type constructors for building complex data structures from elementary schemas, including structs with optional/mutable keys, tuples, arrays, records, and discriminated unions.

Key Concepts:

Structs:
- Schema.Struct({ key: SchemaType }) for object schemas
- Schema.optionalKey(schema) for `key?: T` (key may be absent)
- Schema.optional(schema) for `key?: T | undefined` (key present but undefined allowed)
- Schema.mutableKey(schema) for mutable fields
- Default values via Schema.withDefault(() => value) or Schema.withConstructorDefault(() => value)
- Index signatures via Schema.indexSignature(keySchema, valueSchema)
- Struct field manipulation: .mapFields(Struct.pick(["a"])), .mapFields(Struct.omit(["b"])), .mapFields(Struct.map(Schema.optional))
- Schema.fieldsAssign({ newField: Schema.String }) to extend structs
- Schema.annotate({ description, title }) for metadata
- Schema.annotateKey({ description, missingKeyMessage }) for per-field metadata

Tuples:
- Schema.Tuple([Schema.String, Schema.Number]) - array syntax in v4
- Optional elements, rest elements supported

Arrays:
- Schema.Array(elementSchema) for ReadonlyArray
- Schema.NonEmptyArray(elementSchema)

Records:
- Schema.Record(keySchema, valueSchema) - positional args in v4 (not named object)

Unions:
- Schema.Union([Schema.String, Schema.Number]) - array syntax in v4
- Tagged unions with Schema.tag("TypeName") auto-discriminated by _tag
- Schema.TaggedClass for discriminated union variants

Since: effect v4 beta

name: Effect v4 Function: Effect.gen
episode_body:
Title: Effect.gen
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Effect
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Creating Effects

Description: Provides a way to write effectful code using generator functions, simplifying control flow and error handling. **When to Use** `gen` allows you to write code that looks and behaves like synchronous code, but it can handle asynchronous tasks, errors, and complex control flow (like loops and conditions). It helps make asynchronous code more readable and easier to manage. The generator functions work similarly to `async/await` but with more explicit control over the execution of effects. You can `y

Signature: gen: { <Eff extends Yieldable<any, any, any, any>, AEff>( f: () => Generator<Eff, AEff, never> ): Effect< AEff,

Example:
```ts
import { Effect } from "effect"

const addServiceCharge = (amount: number) => amount + 1

const applyDiscount = (
  total: number,
  discountRate: number
): Effect.Effect<number, Error> =>
  discountRate === 0
    ? Effect.fail(new Error("Discount rate cannot be zero"))
    : Effect.succeed(total - (total * discountRate) / 100)

const fetchTransactionAmount = Effect.promise(() => Promise.resolve(100))

const fetchDiscountRate = Effect.promise(() => Promise.resolve(5))

export const program

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.map
episode_body:
Title: Array.map
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: mapping

Description: Transforms each element using a function, returning a new array. - The function receives `(element, index)`. - Preserves `NonEmptyArray` in the return type.

Signature: map: { <S extends ReadonlyArray<any>, B>( f: (a: ReadonlyArray.Infer<S>, i: number) => B ): (self: S) => ReadonlyArray.With<S, B> <S extends ReadonlyArray<any>, B>(self: S, f: (a: ReadonlyArray.Infer<S>, i: number) => B): ReadonlyArray.With<S, B>

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

console.log(Array.map([1, 2, 3], (x) => x * 2)) // [2, 4, 6]
```

Since: 2.0.0
